summary: BEG (Leverage Shares 2X Long BE Daily ETF) has delivered massive short-term gains in mid-April 2026 with multiple days of 40%+ gains (e.g. +42.75% and +46.3%), amplifying moves in Bloom Energy (BE) stock after its expanded Oracle partnership for fuel cell technology.[[1]](https://x.com/etfbeacon/status/2044258249352839556)[[2]](https://x.com/tradingcoaot6/status/2044405116137066625) Social media is dominated by momentum traders listing it among top performers, though some voices warn of overvaluation in BE and advise caution after the rapid run-up.[[3]](https://x.com/dylanstonkss/status/2045186405769392478) The UK insolvency firm Begbies Traynor (also BEG) shows steadier positive sentiment around its rebrand to BTG and growth initiatives. Overall near-term financial sentiment is positive but highly speculative given the leveraged product's risks.[[4]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AGIx0vDJ3CM)
sources: 10+ recent X posts documenting 40%+ daily moves, News on Bloom Energy Oracle deal and Q1 2026 earnings, ETF performance data (YTD >300% moves), Begbies Traynor (LON:BEG) rebranding updates
